question: What are the criteria that must be assessed in order to qualify a creation as a 'work' within the meaning of EU law?
topic: Copyright — autonomous EU concept of 'work' — originality and identifiability (Directive 2001/29)
gold_answer: Two cumulative conditions. First, an original subject matter that is the author's own intellectual creation — original meaning it reflects the author's personality through free and creative choices; if dictated by technical considerations leaving no creative freedom, it is not original. Second, the 'work' must be an expression identifiable with sufficient precision and objectivity (Cofemel, C-683/17, paras 29-32; Infopaq, C-5/08, paras 37, 39; Levola Hengelo, C-310/17, para 40).
source: Amicus_3.docx — expert legal memo (section VIII), provided 2026-06-02
notes: corpus_gap: ALL three controlling copyright cases (Cofemel, Infopaq, Levola) are ABSENT from the corpus and absent from the citation graph (in_degree 0). Indicates a systematic copyright/IP coverage gap. Excluded from the main aggregate; documents the gap.
